title: M1 verification batch 6 of N — 5 narrow text patches + 3 OBE closes
target: WIN-001 detail_evidence (two narrow sentence inserts), WIN-038 detail_evidence (one verbatim-quote insert), WIN-010 detail_verdict_text (one softening + one caveat sentence), part5 SEC-5.0 (one Lakatos gloss sentence). Plus 3 OBE closures (ISS-1043/1549/1220) where no analyst patch is appropriate.
summary_for_decider: Sixth verification batch under the M1-orphan drain HNOTE. 5 PATCH operations (PATCH-307-A..E) close 5 orphan ISSs with narrow text corrections that don't introduce new arguments; 3 OBE closures (ISS-1043 monitoring-tag-pre-eclipse-correct, ISS-1549 reassign-to-tinker-build-infra, ISS-1220 css-audit-confirms-already-refuted-styled). All 5 patches are verification-class — they are word-level / single-sentence-insert refinements to existing arguments. Combined with EXP-302..306 progress entries, ~48 of original 61-83 orphan ISSs are now drained across six analyst runs. HNOTE remains status=pending per multi-cycle directive.

patches.old_string: The closing tell is the ratio itself: v_a/c = 1.574 = 20,015 / 12,717 = r_disc / disc_thickness. The dome's two "different" derivations are the same derivation; v_a was chosen precisely so the live-page numbers reproduce the wins.html numbers.
patches.new_string: The closing tell is the ratio itself: v_a/c = 1.574 = 20,015 / 12,717 = r_disc / disc_thickness. The 0.2% offset between v_a/c = 1.574 and the textbook πc/2-over-c value 1.5708 is exactly the offset between the dome's disc_thickness = 12,717 km and Earth's diameter 2 × R<sub>Earth</sub> = 12,742 km — v_a was fit to absorb that residual, so f = v_a / (2 × r_disc) and f = c / (2 × disc_thickness) agree at 11.787 Hz by construction, not by independent physics. The dome's two "different" derivations are the same derivation; v_a was chosen precisely so the live-page numbers reproduce the wins.html numbers.
patches.rationale: ISS-1315 asked for an in-situ explanation of the 0.2% offset in the formula-collapse chain. The new sentence shows the v_a/c ratio is offset from the textbook πc/2 / c value by exactly the same 0.2% that disc_thickness is offset from 2×R_earth — making the by-construction agreement visible.
patches.verification_class_justification: Closes a defender pivot by explicitly naming a numerical relationship that was already implicit in the surrounding prose. No new argument, no new citation, no new section.

patches.old_string:
This is the diagnostic signature of per-WIN parameter fitting, not unified physical modeling.

Tesla's location destroys the dome interpretation.
patches.new_string:
This is the diagnostic signature of per-WIN parameter fitting, not unified physical modeling. A dispersion-law defense — "different modes on a dispersive medium have different phase velocities, so c for WIN-029 and v_a for WIN-001 can both be right on the same circuit" — fails on its own terms: the dome publishes no dispersion relation ω(k), no mode index, and no cavity eigenvalue calculation that would justify the per-WIN speed selection. The c-vs-v_a choice is operational (whatever number reproduces the published Hz value), not theoretical (a mode formula's output).

Tesla's location destroys the dome interpretation.
patches.rationale: ISS-1316 asked for an in-situ dispersion-law preempt so a defender cannot retreat behind "dispersive medium." The added sentence names the retreat and closes it by pointing out the absence of any published dispersion relation, mode index, or eigenvalue calculation that would license per-WIN speed selection.
patches.verification_class_justification: Single-sentence preempt of a specific defender retreat. Argument structure unchanged; the existing "Same circuit, two incompatible wave speeds" subsection already establishes the inconsistency — this just denies the dispersive-medium escape.

patches.old_string: WIN-029 is the credit claim for that exact inversion. A retreat to transverse modes does not save WIN-038 in isolation
patches.new_string: WIN-029 is the credit claim for that exact inversion. The dome's wins.html scorecard records this in its own words: "WIN-029 — Schumann Resonance Requires Hard Conductive Ceiling CONFIRMED (H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model)." The quarter-wave formula is framed by the dome itself as the equation that produces the confirmation — not as a simplified heuristic to be set aside. A retreat to transverse modes does not save WIN-038 in isolation
patches.rationale: ISS-1340 asked for the verbatim WIN-029 scorecard quote to be inserted near the 5.3% agreement cite, preempting the "quarter-wave is just a simplified heuristic" retreat. Quoting the dome verbatim closes that retreat because the dome itself frames quarter-wave as the confirming equation.
patches.verification_class_justification: Citation-precision addition — quotes existing dome scorecard text already referenced. No new argument; just makes the existing point harder to retreat from by quoting the source verbatim.

patches.old_string: fits the pattern Lakatos (1970, Falsification and the Methodology of Scientific Research Programmes) identified as a degenerative research programme — auxiliary conditions tightened to protect the programme from observations that have gone wrong, without generating new predictive content;
patches.new_string: fits the pattern Lakatos (1970, Falsification and the Methodology of Scientific Research Programmes) identified as a degenerative research programme — auxiliary conditions tightened to protect the programme from observations that have gone wrong, without generating new predictive content. The mapping to Lakatos's canonical pattern (where defenders add auxiliary hypotheses, as in the Prout-Bohr case) is direct: a falsification rule narrowed after observation to exclude exactly the observations that fell foul of the old rule is functionally equivalent to introducing a new auxiliary hypothesis — "rows of this kind do not falsify the model" — solely to protect the programme. The structural move is the same; only the surface form (rule-tightening vs hypothesis-adding) differs;
patches.rationale: ISS-1407 said the Lakatos citation was rhetorically vulnerable because the canonical degenerative-programme example (Prout-Bohr) is auxiliary-hypothesis ADDITION, not rule TIGHTENING. A defender can argue the dome's move is not Lakatosian. The added gloss sentence shows the two are functionally equivalent — narrowing a rule to exclude embarrassing observations IS the same move as adding "those observations are explained by [new auxiliary]." Same structural move, different surface form.
patches.verification_class_justification: One-sentence gloss that strengthens an existing citation without changing the underlying argument. Curmudgeon explicitly recommended this as one of two acceptable fixes ("add a one-sentence gloss explaining how relabeling maps to the auxiliary-hypothesis pattern").

patches.old_string: The scaling shape is also not uniquely dome-geometric: a broad family of smooth monotonic H(r) functions fitted to the same sun/moon altitude calibration targets, combined with the dome's r·tan(θ) = H(r) convention, produces a latitude-dependent ratio of effectively the same form over the relevant r range, so matching one parametrization to 0.1% does not discriminate the dome from any alternative disc-geometry model with a comparable height function.
patches.new_string: The scaling shape is also not uniquely dome-geometric: alternative smooth monotonically-decreasing H(r) functions calibrated to the same two anchors (sun altitude at r_sun and moon altitude at r_moon), combined with the dome's r·tan(θ) = H(r) convention, should produce closely-similar FSF curves over the dome's 0–14,000 km station-radius range, because H(r)/r over a finite range is constrained primarily by the calibration anchors rather than the specific interpolating family. We have not exhaustively swept alternative parametrizations (Gaussian, power-law, piecewise-linear, etc.) against the nine-station FSF table to confirm this in detail; the dome, however, has not demonstrated that the exponential form is uniquely required either. Matching one parametrization to 0.1% therefore does not discriminate the dome from any alternative disc-geometry model with a comparable height function — but the strength of the non-discrimination claim is bounded by the (still open) exhaustivity check.
patches.rationale: ISS-1438 / WIN-010 c6 H3 (deferred from EXP-228): the universal "any smooth monotonic H(r)" claim was unsupported. The softer phrasing (a) replaces "any smooth monotonic" with "alternative smooth monotonically-decreasing... should produce", (b) explicitly notes the exhaustivity check has not been done on our side, (c) flags that the dome has not done the inverse demonstration either, and (d) caveats the strength of the non-discrimination conclusion accordingly.
patches.verification_class_justification: Per curmudgeon recommendation (option (b): softer phrasing), replaces the universal with a bounded claim plus explicit caveat. No new argument, no new evidence — just phrasing precision and an honesty caveat.

obe_closures.reason: No analyst-write action needed pre-eclipse (2026-08-12). WIN-044.code_analysis.monitoring is currently "none"; the issue's own monitoring_evidence text already concludes "'none' is correct" for the pre-event state, because monitor.py Domain 10 has not yet fetched 2026 INTERMAGNET data (the event has not occurred). The recommended-update-to-"hardcoded"-tag is post-eclipse housekeeping that will be triggered automatically when monitor.py begins consuming the August 2026 data on/after 2026-08-12 — and the routing-time annotation in the issue itself ("Defer update until eclipse day (2026-08-12) when observed slot is populated; tag should update automatically post-eclipse") explicitly classifies it as a wait-state, not an analyst-write task. Closing as OBE: post-eclipse-automation, not analyst work.

EXP-211 AC8/9/10/12 (Playwright viewport-fold reachability + a11y snapshot) are Playwright integration-test work, not analyst work. test.js has no Playwright plumbing today; adding it is build-infrastructure (tinker territory) — not new-argument or text-revision (analyst territory). Curmudgeon's own review (EXP-211-redesign-v2-proposal.json H6) offered three options: (a) extend test.js with Playwright assertions, (b) document measured DOMrect values as evidence, (c) downgrade to static layout assertions. All three are mechanical-test-engineering tasks. Reassign to tinker for AC8/9/10/12 disposition under PROP-014 or equivalent infra-tracking. Closing as OBE: reassign-to-tinker.

CSS audit performed: docs/index.html line 214 defines `.ks-status.ks-claimed{background:var(--refuted);color:var(--refuted-solid)}`. The `ks-claimed` class uses the refuted (red) color tokens, NOT green/check-mark styling as curmudgeon hypothesized. Inspecting the rendered output: the demoted SEC-5.0 rows (Sydney-Perth, Polaris, and the explicit "demoted 2026-04-17" structural-support rows at lines 3291/3297) all carry .ks-status.ks-claimed and render in refuted-red as the design already intends. The curmudgeon's "likely renders as green/check-mark" hypothesis was inverted — the existing CSS already signals "dome claims this but our verdict disagrees." No CSS change needed. Closing as OBE: css-audit-found-no-issue. (Note: build-scripts/generate-html.js line 668 emits the same rule, so the in-repo source and the rendered output match.)

throughput_note: 8 ISSs cleared this run (5 PATCH + 3 OBE) — at upper end of HNOTE 5-10 target. Combined with prior batches (EXP-302..306 cleared 7+6+8+8+11 = 40 ISSs), cumulative ~48 of original 61-83 orphan ISSs drained across six analyst runs.

notes_to_curmudgeon: All 5 patches are word/sentence-level inserts into existing argument scaffolds. Recommend Step 8a batch review.

kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-307-A: The 0.2% offset is a real numerical fact; the dome's v_a was indeed chosen to absorb it. Acknowledging this explicitly strengthens the by-construction critique without strawmanning.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-307-B: A dispersive medium genuinely DOES support different phase velocities for different modes. The defender pivot is real — what the patch denies is that the dome has actually invoked or computed any such dispersion relation. The kernel ("dispersive media exist") is true; the kernel does not save the dome.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-307-C: WIN-029 IS a genuine numerical agreement to 5.3% — the dome got the inversion arithmetic right. The verbatim quote makes that clear, then shows the agreement is precisely what makes a quarter-wave retreat impossible.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-307-D: Lakatos's canonical case IS hypothesis-addition, not rule-tightening. The defender pivot is real — granted explicitly in the new gloss. The patch shows the two moves are structurally equivalent rather than denying the surface difference.
kernel_of_truth_per_patch.PATCH-307-E: A broad-family alternative-H(r) demonstration would be more convincing than the current claim — granted. The softened phrasing acknowledges we have not done that work, while pointing out the dome has not done the inverse uniqueness proof either. Both sides bounded.
